_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the role and responses of international, national, and non-government organisations in addressing differences in human wellbeing.

_source.w: We are learning about the role and responses of international, national, and non-government organisations in addressing differences in human wellbeing.
y: Organisations at every scale work to improve human wellbeing — understanding their roles helps us evaluate their effectiveness.
These organisations shape the global response to inequality, poverty, and human rights — their work affects billions.
This knowledge helps us identify how we can contribute to improving wellbeing through supporting or engaging with these organisations.
eg: Investigating the roles of international organisations like the UN, World Bank, or WHO in addressing wellbeing inequalities.
Examining the work of national government agencies in improving domestic wellbeing outcomes.
Analysing the contributions of non-government organisations like UNICEF, Oxfam, or local community organisations.
Evaluating the effectiveness of aid programs in improving wellbeing in developing countries.
Discussing the debate about whether aid creates dependency or genuinely improves long-term outcomes.
Comparing the approaches of different organisations to the same wellbeing challenge and evaluating their relative effectiveness.
